summary: The US and Iran agreed to halt mutual attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and plan to meet Tuesday in Doha, though Iran subsequently canceled scheduled technical talks citing recent attacks and unfulfilled conditions. The IDF destroyed a large Hezbollah tunnel in southern Lebanon's Majdal Zoun area containing hundreds of weapons and four launchers aimed at Israel, while also eliminating senior Hamas commanders in Gaza. Iran's IRGC claimed destruction of eight US military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain using ballistic missiles and drones in retaliation for American strikes on Qeshm and Sirri.
key_points: The US and Iran agreed to stop attacking each other and plan to meet Tuesday in Doha to resolve their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4488)
Iran canceled scheduled technical talks with the US, citing recent attacks and unfulfilled conditions as the reason for non-participation. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4484)

IDF destroyed a Hezbollah tunnel in Majdal Zoun, southern Lebanon, over 200 meters long and 25 meters deep, containing hundreds of weapons and four launchers aimed at Israel. (Source: @idf_telegram/24466)
IDF and Shin Bet killed Mansour Sami Mahmoud Shhata, commander of Hamas's Central Camps maritime police, and two other Hamas commanders in central Gaza. (Source: @almogboker78/17620)

IRGC claimed joint naval and aerial operations destroyed eight US military infrastructure targets at Kuwait's Ali Al-Salem base and Bahrain's Fifth Fleet using ballistic missiles and drones. (Source: @SharghDaily/168648)

IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir expressed support for the Lebanon agreement, calling it historic and significant, while approving future operational plans at Northern Command. (Source: @amitsegal/62595)

Saudi Arabia intercepted missiles over northern Jordan, and sirens were heard in Bahrain and Kuwait following IRGC attacks on US bases in those countries. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80258, @asafroz/50810)
IDF identified the Hezbollah militant who killed Sgt. Maj. David Hazut in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, and eliminated him in a follow-up operation overnight. (Source: @pkpoi/143020)

sections.executive_summary: The US and Iran reached a preliminary agreement to halt mutual attacks in the Strait of Hormuz with a planned meeting in Doha on Tuesday, though Iran subsequently canceled technical talks citing unfulfilled conditions. The IDF conducted major operations in Lebanon destroying a Hezbollah tunnel with hundreds of weapons and in Gaza eliminating senior Hamas commanders. Iran's IRGC launched retaliatory strikes on US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, while Saudi Arabia intercepted missiles over northern Jordan.

points: Iran agreed with the US to stop attacking each other and planned to meet Tuesday in Doha to resolve their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, according to Axios. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4488)
Iran canceled scheduled technical talks with the US, with an Iranian official telling state TV the cancellation was due to recent attacks and unfulfilled conditions. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4484)
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned against any interference in Iran's management of the Strait of Hormuz and arrangements for its reopening. (Source: @presstv/196039)

IRGC claimed joint naval and aerial operations destroyed eight US military infrastructure targets at Kuwait's Ali Al-Salem base and Bahrain's Fifth Fleet using ballistic missiles and drones, in retaliation for US strikes on Qeshm and Sirri. (Source: @SharghDaily/168648)

Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz dropped dramatically, with approximately 50 oil tankers and 40 containers crossing in June 2026 compared to 1,150 tankers and 400 containers in December 2025. (Source: @SharghDaily/168642)

US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz warned that Trump's patience with Iran is running out, stating Iran's continued attacks on international shipping will not go unanswered. (Source: @SharghDaily/168639, @pkpoi/143022)

IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir expressed support for the Lebanon agreement, calling it historic and significant, while approving future operational plans at Northern Command. (Source: @amitsegal/62595, @manniefabian/58696)

IDF destroyed a Hezbollah tunnel in Majdal Zoun, southern Lebanon, over 200 meters long and 25 meters deep, containing hundreds of weapons and four launchers aimed at Israeli territory. (Source: @idf_telegram/24466)

IDF and Shin Bet killed Mansour Sami Mahmoud Shhata, commander of Hamas's Central Camps maritime police, and two other Hamas commanders in central Gaza who promoted terror plots against IDF forces. (Source: @almogboker78/17620, @inon_yttach/9189)

Sgt. Maj. David Hazut, 21, from Ashkelon, a platoon commander in Battalion 12, Golani Brigade, was killed in combat in southern Lebanon when his tank was hit by a drone bomb. (Source: @New_security8200/202131, @inon_yttach/9199)
IDF identified and eliminated the Hezbollah militant responsible for killing Sgt. Maj. David Hazut in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, during a follow-up operation overnight. (Source: @pkpoi/143020)

Western Galilee residents were warned of a high-intensity explosion as the military prepared to demolish a large Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon. (Source: @manniefabian/58699, @N12chat/215076)
Israeli police and Shin Bet arrested a Tanzim activist and arms dealer in the Balata refugee camp in the Shomron Brigade area who planned an imminent attack. (Source: @yediotnews25/132023)

IDF killed Abd al-Rahman Maher Abd al-Karim Ziyada, a Nukhba Force cell commander in Hamas's military wing, in an airstrike on Gaza City's Shati Camp; he had infiltrated Israel during the October 7 massacre and looted an IDF vehicle. (Source: @manniefabian/58693)
A separate strike in Khan Younis, southern Gaza, killed Kamal Muhammad Hamdan Najar, head of the tunnel unit in the Khan Younis area, described as a Hamas tunnel expert. (Source: @manniefabian/58693)

Senior IDF intelligence officials warned Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir that Hamas is rebuilding for war, producing IEDs and anti-tank missiles, recruiting young fighters, and restoring tunnels. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4479)

Israeli police, Shin Bet, and the Egoz Unit arrested a Tanzim activist and arms dealer in Balata refugee camp who planned an imminent attack against security forces and civilians. (Source: @yediotnews25/132023)

IDF destroyed a Hezbollah tunnel in Majdal Zoun, southern Lebanon, over 200 meters long and 25 meters deep, containing hundreds of weapons and four launchers aimed at Israeli territory, built using Iranian technology and knowledge. (Source: @idf_telegram/24466)
Sgt. Maj. David Hazut was killed and another soldier lightly injured when their tank was hit by a drone bomb during combat in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon. (Source: @inon_yttach/9199, @pkpoi/143020)

IDF Chief of Staff Zamir called the Lebanon agreement historic and significant, stating the military is set to partially withdraw as part of the deal while honoring its terms. (Source: @manniefabian/58696, @amitsegal/62595)
Western Galilee residents were warned of a high-intensity explosion as the military prepared to demolish a large Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon. (Source: @manniefabian/58699)
Several airstrikes struck southern Lebanon, according to reports from the area. (Source: @shin_persian/7286)
Israeli Apache helicopters targeted with machine gun fire the village of Abidin in western Daraa countryside, Syria. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373467)

Iraqi security forces conducted widespread operations in Wasit province, with security elements searching homes of arrested officials in Baghdad and other Iraqi cities. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80269, @naya_foriraq/80231)

Erbil handed over eight detainees to Iraq's Integrity Commission at Alton Kupri checkpoint, including parliamentarians Muhammad Al-Mayahi, Ashwaq Al-Jabouri, and Ziyad Al-Janabi, plus five senior government employees. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80255)

IRGC claimed joint naval and aerial operations destroyed eight US military infrastructure targets at Kuwait's Ali Al-Salem base and Bahrain's Fifth Fleet using ballistic missiles and drones. (Source: @SharghDaily/168648, @kianmeli1/88217)
Saudi Arabia intercepted missiles over northern Jordan, according to Saudi media reports. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80258)
Sirens were heard in Bahrain and Kuwait following IRGC attacks on US bases in those countries in retaliation for American strikes on Iran. (Source: @asafroz/50810)
A Saudi Aramco helicopter crashed in Ras Tanura, killing 14 people. (Source: @presstv/196048)

key_developments: US and Iran agreed to halt mutual attacks in the Strait of Hormuz with a planned Doha meeting Tuesday, though Iran subsequently canceled technical talks citing recent attacks and unfulfilled conditions. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4488, @Faytuks_Network/4484)

IDF destroyed a Hezbollah tunnel in Majdal Zoun, southern Lebanon, over 200 meters long and 25 meters deep, containing hundreds of weapons and four launchers aimed at Israeli territory, built using Iranian technology. (Source: @idf_telegram/24466)

IRGC claimed destruction of eight US military infrastructure targets at Kuwait's Ali Al-Salem base and Bahrain's Fifth Fleet using ballistic missiles and drones, retaliating for US strikes on Qeshm and Sirri. (Source: @SharghDaily/168648)

IDF and Shin Bet killed Mansour Sami Mahmoud Shhata, commander of Hamas's Central Camps maritime police, and two other Hamas commanders in central Gaza who promoted terror plots against IDF forces. (Source: @almogboker78/17620)

Sgt. Maj. David Hazut, 21, from Ashkelon, Golani Brigade, was killed in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, when his tank was hit by a drone bomb; the responsible Hezbollah militant was later eliminated. (Source: @New_security8200/202131, @pkpoi/143020)

IDF Chief of Staff Zamir called the Lebanon agreement historic and significant, approving future operational plans at Northern Command as the IDF prepares partial withdrawal under the deal. (Source: @manniefabian/58696, @amitsegal/62595)

Saudi Arabia intercepted missiles over northern Jordan, and maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz dropped from 1,150 tankers in December 2025 to approximately 50 in June 2026. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80258, @SharghDaily/168642)
sections.threat_assessment: The situation remains highly volatile with multiple simultaneous escalation vectors. The US-Iran de-escalation agreement over the Strait of Hormuz is fragile, as Iran canceled technical talks and IRGC launched major attacks on US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Hezbollah retains significant tunnel infrastructure in southern Lebanon with weapons aimed at Israel, though the IDF is actively destroying these assets. Hamas continues rebuilding military capabilities including IEDs, anti-tank missiles, and tunnels in Gaza despite targeted killings of senior commanders. The risk of miscalculation remains high across all theaters.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states are directly affected by the US-Iran confrontation, with Saudi Arabia intercepting missiles over Jordan and Bahrain/Kuwait experiencing IRGC attacks on US bases on their territory. Lebanon faces the dual pressure of a new agreement with Israel requiring Hezbollah's withdrawal and ongoing IDF operations in the south. Iraq continues internal security operations with mass arrests of officials on corruption charges. The dramatic reduction in Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic signals severe economic disruption for the entire region.
sections.intelligence_notes: The IRGC claim of destroying eight US bases requires independent verification, as such claims are often exaggerated for propaganda purposes. The destruction of the Majdal Zoun tunnel with Iranian technology confirms continued Iranian support for Hezbollah's military infrastructure despite the Lebanon agreement. Hamas's rebuilding efforts including IED production and tunnel restoration suggest the group is preparing for prolonged conflict despite leadership losses. The cancellation of US-Iran technical talks despite the Doha agreement indicates deep mistrust and the ceasefire may be short-lived. The Saudi Aramco helicopter crash in Ras Tanura warrants monitoring for potential links to regional tensions.
